summary: Excellent matrix recording captures an inspired late‑90s run with standout Dark Star, Eyes of the World and a moving Brokedown Palace.
review: The three fans rave about this matrix source – praised for its crystal‑clear mix and balanced blend of board and audience ambience. Musically the show is highlighted as one of the band’s finest of the era, with soaring Dark Star, an up‑tempo Eyes of the World, a powerful Other One and an emotional Brokedown Palace, despite mixed feelings about Hornsby’s accordion on that latter tune.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are enthusiastic (5, 4, 5 stars); detailed praise for both performance and recording outweighs the single criticism of keyboard texture.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note "pure alchemy" and flawless mastering; the matrix captures ambience without audible fire‑alarm or crowd noise, delivering a crisp, well‑balanced soundstage.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: The band took a relaxed, unhurried approach, building a memorable first set and delivering an explosive second set with superb transitions (e.g., Dark Star → Saint, Other One → Mirror).

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for emotive solos, beautiful tone, and steering the ship throughout the performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for strong interaction, melodic bass lines and helping lift the band's energy.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals and guitar work described as passionate; his “Tibetan‑inspired” sounds added intensity.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby’s accordion/keys drew mixed reactions – some felt it was aggressive and out of place on Brokedown Palace, while other moments were called "soft summer rain" and tasteful.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming described as cerebral, building intensity and providing a solid backbone for the improvisations.
